questions.text: I feel a strong pull to put community needs above my personal desires.
I tend to protect my personal time fiercely.
I find that I navigate social situations best when the rules are clearly defined.
I find that preparation reduces my anxiety and increases my confidence.
I notice when a text message carries a tone that differs from the sender's usual style.
I feel a strong pull to be in charge of important initiatives.
I find that acting in my own interest when I could be helping others feels like a failure.
I prefer a general sense of direction over specific, rigid objectives.
I hold the view that detached, methodical reasoning about painful situations is the approach that yields the broadest view and the most thoughtful response.
I often initiate projects without waiting for approval.
I prefer to be a contributor alongside others rather than the sole architect.
I notice when a group is collectively avoiding a topic that everyone is thinking about.
I believe my nervous system runs at a higher baseline than most people's.
I find that my emotional state changes gradually, like a thermostat rather than a switch.
I feel that the obligation to respond to pain is a commitment that defines the core of my ethical life.
I believe that the moral weight of someone's suffering should never be discounted simply because responding to it is inconvenient.
I find that creating checklists helps me stay focused and avoid overlooking details.
I notice that my face and body betray my emotional intensity before I have a chance to modulate it.
